### kiri approve consume

Validate + consume a use-once token...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri approve consume [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--token` | The plaintext bearer token from `kiri approve issue`. | yes |  |  |
| `--sql-hash` | Canonical SQL fingerprint, ``sha256:<hex>`` shape (matches kiri_core.ai_query_gate.audit.canonical_sql_hash). Mutually exclusive with --sql; pass exactly one. | no |  |  |
| `--sql` | The raw SQL being resubmitted. CLI canonicalises via ``canonical_sql_hash`` before consume. Use this for operator smoke; production callers (BFF, MCP) should pass --sql-hash directly. | no |  |  |
| `--store` | Backend kind (memory\|jsonl\|postgres). | no | jsonl |  |
| `--store-path` | Backend location: filesystem path for 'jsonl', Postgres URL for 'postgres', ignored for 'memory'. | no |  |  |

### kiri approve consume-reconcile

Consume a token bound to an...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri approve consume-reconcile [OPTIONS]
```

None.

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |

| `--store` | Backend kind (memory\|jsonl\|postgres). | no | jsonl |  |

### kiri approve grant

Record an APPROVE vote on a pending request.

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri approve grant [OPTIONS] REQUEST_ID
```

### Arguments

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `REQUEST_ID` | The request id to approve. | yes |  |  |

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--as` | Approver principal (email or group name). | yes |  |  |
| `--rationale` | Free-text justification (optional in light tier). | no |  |  |
| `--store` | Backend kind (memory\|jsonl\|postgres). | no | jsonl |  |
| `--store-path` | Backend location: filesystem path for 'jsonl', Postgres URL for 'postgres', ignored for 'memory'. | no |  |  |

## kiri history

Show Delta commit history for a bronze...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri history [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--target`, `-t` | Named profile to use (dev\|test\|prod). Falls back to KIRIMANA_TARGET or the project's default_target. | no |  |  |
| `--source` | Source name | yes |  |  |
| `--table` | Table name | yes |  |  |
| `--limit` | Max commits to show | no | 10 |  |

## kiri reconcile

Post-migration data validation: source vs...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri reconcile [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--source-target` | MSSQL profile in kiri.yml | no | mssql |  |
| `--target-target` | Databricks profile in kiri.yml | no | databricks |  |
| `--source-table` | Fully-qualified source table | no |  |  |
| `--target-table` | Fully-qualified target table | no |  |  |
| `--primary-key` | Comma-separated PK column list | no |  |  |
| `--columns` | Comma-separated columns for null-rate check | no |  |  |
| `--date-columns` | Comma-separated DATE/TIMESTAMP columns for bounds check | no |  |  |
| `--business-key` | Comma-separated SCD2 business-key columns | no |  |  |
| `--effective-from` | SCD2 effective-from column | no |  |  |
| `--effective-to` | SCD2 effective-to column | no |  |  |
| `--attribute-columns` | Comma-separated REC-104 full-row fingerprint columns (default: --columns) | no |  |  |
| `--source-snapshot-id` | Observed source frozen-batch snapshot id | no |  |  |
| `--source-dialect` | mssql \| spark | no | mssql |  |
| `--target-dialect` | mssql \| spark | no | spark |  |
| `--report-mode` | full \| locked (LOCKED omits raw values) | no |  |  |
| `--pk-mode` | Override reconcile.pk_check_mode: full \| sample \| spot | no |  |  |
| `--sample-size` | Override reconcile.pk_sample_size (0 = use config) | no | 0 |  |
| `--format` | json \| markdown (default: json) | no | json |  |
| `--output`, `-o` | Write the overall report here | no |  |  |
| `--reports-dir` | Per-table report directory (default: <project>/reconciliation) | no |  |  |
| `--write-apply-log`, `--no-apply-log` | Write an _kiri_apply_log row via the target adapter (best-effort; never blocks the report). | no | True |  |

## kiri release

Multi-environment release tracking

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri release [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]...
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

None.

### Subcommands

- [`kiri release apply`](#kiri-release-apply) - Promote a git SHA to ``--to`` and stamp...
- [`kiri release contract`](#kiri-release-contract) - Show every environment's currently-active...
- [`kiri release history`](#kiri-release-history) - Show the most recent promotions, newest...
- [`kiri release init`](#kiri-release-init) - Populate the release-state store with...
- [`kiri release matrix`](#kiri-release-matrix) - Show which contract version is live in...
- [`kiri release plan`](#kiri-release-plan) - Diff HEAD (or --from) against the commit...
- [`kiri release status`](#kiri-release-status) - Show which commit SHA is live in every...

### kiri release apply

Promote a git SHA to ``--to`` and stamp...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri release apply [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--to` | Target environment (dev\|test\|prod\|…) | yes |  |  |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--sha` | Git SHA to promote. Defaults to HEAD. If provided, must resolve to the same commit as HEAD - this command does not checkout; the caller (CI or a human) is responsible for having the right ref checked out. | no |  |  |
| `--tag` | Optional annotated tag (e.g. v1.2.0) stamped into the manifest. | no |  |  |
| `--allow-dirty` | Promote despite uncommitted changes (dev-only escape hatch). | no | False |  |
| `--skip-dbt` | Skip the silver/gold dbt build step | no | False |  |
| `--skip-fetch` | Skip implicit REST/DATABASE fetch (reuse existing landing files). | no | False |  |
| `--domain` | Promote only contracts in the named domain. Same scoping semantics as ``kiri apply --domain``: dbt build and legacy-source fallback are skipped; release_sha still stamps the project manifest as a whole. | no |  |  |

### kiri release contract

Show every environment's currently-active...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri release contract [OPTIONS] URN
```

### Arguments

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `URN` | Contract URN, e.g. 'orders.silver'. | yes |  |  |

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--output`, `-o` | Output format: table (default) or json (for CI / bots) | no | table |  |
| `--database-url` | Postgres or DuckDB URL. | no |  |  |

### kiri release history

Show the most recent promotions, newest...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri release history [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--env` | Filter to one environment (omit for all). | no |  |  |
| `--limit` | Maximum number of rows to show. | no | 20 |  |
| `--output`, `-o` | Output format: table (default) or json (for CI / bots) | no | table |  |
| `--database-url` | Postgres or DuckDB URL. | no |  |  |

### kiri release plan

Diff HEAD (or --from) against the commit...

### Usage

```text
Usage: kiri release plan [OPTIONS]
```

### Arguments

None.

### Options

| Name | Description | Required | Default | Choices |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `--to` | Target environment (dev\|test\|prod\|…) | yes |  |  |
| `--project`, `-p` | Project directory | no |  |  |
| `--from` | New state to diff against the env's manifest release_sha. Defaults to HEAD. Accepts any git ref (short SHA, tag, branch name). | no |  |  |
| `--allow-dirty` | Suppress the uncommitted-changes warning (dev-only escape). | no | False |  |
| `--domain` | Restrict the plan to one information domain. Paths under ``contracts/<domain>/`` are kept; medallion-layer subfolders are traversed transparently. kiri.yml / sources/ / models/ entries are dropped since they aren't domain-scoped. | no |  |  |
